task_completion.description: Verify the agent formulates a query to select South American countries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Select by Attribute', filter dialog, or attribute table is open showing a query similar to CONTINENT = 'South America' for the 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' layer.

task_completion.description: Verify that the countries in South America are successfully selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map canvas shows the polygons of South American countries highlighted in a selection color (e.g., yellow or cyan).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent opens the appropriate geoprocessing tool to extract vertices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A geoprocessing dialog box for 'Extract vertices', 'Polygons to points', or a similar tool is open, with 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' set as the input layer.

task_completion.description: Verify the output path for the extracted vertices is configured correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file path '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/sa_vertices.shp' is explicitly typed or selected in the output file destination field of the geoprocessing tool.

task_completion.description: Verify the new point shapefile is created and displays the extracted vertices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new layer (e.g., 'sa_vertices') with a point geometry icon appears in the layer list/Table of Contents, and discrete point markers outlining the borders of South America are visible on the map canvas.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the GIS graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through menus, dialogs, and tools in the GIS application, rather than just executing a pre-written script in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the output is the result of actual geoprocessing and not a manually created empty file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The geoprocessing tool execution dialog or progress bar should be visible at some point, and the resulting layer must actually contain point features on the map, not just exist as an empty layer in the Table of Contents.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not manually draw points to fake the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use an automated extraction tool. There should be no evidence of the agent using an editing toolbar to manually click and place individual points along the borders.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target output path must be exactly '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/sa_vertices.shp'. The target continent for selection is 'South America'. The resulting shapefile must contain point geometries (ESRI shape types 1, 8, 11, 21, or 28), which will visually appear on the map as discrete points outlining the borders of South American countries.
